other_observers: landowner saw the bird, without binoculars.
description: The reason, I'm documenting this bird, was it was the suckleyi subspecies from the Pacific North West.  I think this subspecies is rare in Colorado, though I'm not sure how rare.  The Merlin was quite dark (see photos), was eating on some rodent.  I was driving along Harmony Drive, south of my house in Pueblo West, when I found this bird, in someones front yard tree.  I spent two minutes photographing it.  The landowner was nice, asking what I was photographing, and seemed okay that I was photographing a bird.  I mentioned to the landowner, it was a Merlin, and she saw it.  I didn't mention that it was a rare bird around here.  I've never seen this sub-species around Pueblo.  I think I remember seeing a dark Merlin in SE Colorado, a long time ago.
photographer: Photographer: Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival
